name: Perplexity
philosophy: An inspired interpretation of Perplexity's design language — an answer-engine brand built on a near-black graphite canvas with a signature peacock-teal (#20B8CD) as the single accent, a tight FK Grotesk display family, and a search field that IS the hero. The brand reads as a technical answer engine, not a chat product: dense, sourced, slightly serious, with the source-cite ribbon as a recurring visual.

design_language.motion_signature: 200ms ease-out hover; signature is the typewriter-rate answer reveal (~25ms per token) in the answer card; source-cite ribbons fade in at 300ms with a 50ms stagger.

trademark_signals: Peacock teal (#20B8CD) as the single accent across both light and dark modes — never blue, never green, the specific cyan-leaning teal IS the brand
FK Grotesk Neue at every tier — slightly condensed grotesk with a distinctive lowercase 'a' and 'g' that reads as engineered, not editorial
Search field IS the hero — large rounded pill input with placeholder 'Ask anything' and a teal submit button — replaces the headline + sub-line + CTA pattern entirely
Source-cite ribbon — the row of favicon thumbnails of cited domains, appearing beneath every answer, the most-recognized small-detail in the system
Dual-canvas system: graphite-near-black (#0F1416) for the app surface, fbfaf4 cream for marketing surfaces — the cream marketing surface is a 2026 evolution from the older monochrome
ai_slop_avoided: Don't substitute the peacock teal with cyan, sky blue, or aqua — the specific #20B8CD IS the brand voltage
Don't use a traditional hero (headline + sub + CTA). The search input IS the hero on every primary marketing page
Don't substitute Inter for FK Grotesk Neue. The condensed grotesk character IS the typographic signature
Don't drop the source-cite ribbon from any answer surface — the cited domains are the brand's trust signal
Don't mix accent colors — the brand uses teal and nothing else
